action.content:
Expand the riddle into a story with a funny tone:

What can you catch but not throw?
A cold

Use * to highlight at least 2 sections in your text. For example: *this is a highlighted text section*.
action.label: context

action.content:
Barnaby was a man who took the phrase "catch a cold" quite literally. While everyone else treated the common cold as a metaphorical sneeze of bad luck, Barnaby treated it like a baseball game. He spent his entire winter wardrobe collection in a desperate, futile attempt to *catch a cold before the flu season even had a chance to show up*.

He stood in the middle of the grocery store parking lot, shivering violently in a t-shirt, holding a thermometer like a bat. "Come on," he whispered to the biting wind. "Just one little sniffle. Just a tickle in the throat. I'm ready for you!"

A passerby stopped, looking concerned. "Sir, it's forty degrees out here. You're going to freeze, not get sick."

Barnaby waved him off, adjusting his imaginary catcher's mitt. "Nonsense! I'm a professional. I've studied the trajectory of germs. They love a warm, sweaty, slightly confused host. I am the perfect target."

Sure enough, within an hour, Barnaby felt the first twinge. His nose began to run, and his eyes watered. He cheered, pumping his fist. "Gotcha! You're mine!"

But then, the real problem set in. Barnaby realized he had successfully *caught a cold that refused to leave his body no matter how hard he tried to throw it away*. He tried sneezing it out the window, blowing it into a fan, and even attempting to spit it into a trash can, but the virus was stubborn. It seemed to have taken up residence in his sinuses, refusing to be tossed aside like a crumpled receipt.

By Tuesday, Barnaby was a miserable mess, wrapped in three blankets, muttering to himself, "I can't throw this away! It's stuck! It's like trying to throw a wet sock at a wall; it just bounces right back!" His friends tried to help by bringing him soup, but Barnaby just shook his head, holding up a thermometer. "No, no, I need to practice my throwing technique. Maybe if I sneeze harder, I can launch this thing out of my system."

Eventually, a doctor visited, looking exhausted. "Barnaby, you have a cold. It's a virus. You can't throw it. You just have to wait for your immune system to win the game."

Barnaby sighed, finally accepting his fate as the ultimate victim of a riddle he couldn't solve. "So, I'm stuck with it?"

"Yes," the doctor said gently. "Until it goes away on its own."

Barnaby nodded solemnly, pulling his blanket tighter. "Well, at least I caught it. Now I just have to hope it doesn't decide to stay for the whole season."
action.label: response
